name: Tom Malone
abstract: Tom "Bones" Malone (born June 16, 1947) is an American jazz musician, arranger, and producer. As his nickname implies, he specializes on the trombone but he also plays saxophone, trumpet, tuba, flute, and bass guitar.

interviews.title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: This is a funny but interesting band: Randy Brecker, Barry Rogers, Dave Taylor, Tom Malone, you, Sato, Ryo Kawasaki, Francisco Santino on bass, and Harvey Mason on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Al Chez
about_quotes.evidence: Tom Malone was playing trumpet and trombone... I get hired and I do the three weeks, and it was the last day of sweeps and on the air David Letterman looks over and he goes, “Paul, the new trumpet player you know, sounds great”
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Chez interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, February 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Hey
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Tom 'Bones' Malone; Interviewee agrees with the sentiment but does not mention a personal or professional relationship with Malone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Hey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Marini
about_quotes.evidence: And then I’m looking out and I see my, you know Rubin and Howard Johnson and Cranshaw and the guys, Malone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Marini interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: Maria and Tom Malone had written some charts on Police tunes like “Synchronicity” and “Roxanne.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Shaffer
about_quotes.evidence: luckily Tom was not booked and was able to write the key arrangements for me that we needed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Shaffer interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 24, 2023
